title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During an investigation into the murder of Eleanor Marshall, Superintendent Weston questions Kenneth Marshall regarding his wife's activities and her relationship with Patrick Redfern. Marshall claims he found his wife's room empty after breakfast and subsequently searched for her on the beach. Weston notes that hotel guests observed Eleanor and Redfern becoming inseparable, a fact Marshall dismisses as gossip, claiming he does not interfere with his wife's habits. Hercule Poirot argues that understanding Eleanor's personality is essential to identifying her killer, noting that Marshall has provided very little information about her character.

When asked to account for his morning, Marshall states that after swimming, he returned to the hotel at approximately 10:40. He claims he spent the hour from 10:50 to 11:50 in his room writing emails. To support this, he offers three unstamped letters containing financial documents and lists as evidence, suggesting the sound of his typewriter might have been heard by a nearby maid. He then notes he played tennis at 12:00 with Mrs. Redfern, Miss Darnley, and Mr. Gardner.

Weston further inquires about Eleanor's inheritance. Marshall states that Eleanor had no close relatives and had not written a will, which would leave her entire estate to him. He reveals that Eleanor became a wealthy woman after receiving a bequest of approximately £50,000 from her late friend, Sir Roger Erskine. This revelation prompts Inspector Corrigan to question Marshall's assertion regarding the lack of a will.
